name: [BUG-OT-002] TC-OT-010 — GET /api/v1/token: Invalid Bearer token returns 200 (auth NOT enforced)
description:
**[KNOWN BUG — BUG-OT-002] — CRITICAL SECURITY VULNERABILITY**

**What this test does:**
Sends an intentionally malformed Bearer token ("invalid.token.here") to GET /api/v1/token and verifies the server rejects it with 401.

**Expected:** HTTP 401 Unauthorized
**Actual:** HTTP 200 OK — returns a valid KCB API access_token!

**Root Cause:** The oauth-token service does NOT verify the incoming RMS JWT. It accepts any non-empty string as a Bearer token and returns the KCB API token regardless.

**Impact (Critical):** The KCB OAuth token is obtainable by any caller who sends any string in the Authorization header. This bypasses the entire authentication layer and exposes a sensitive third-party token to unauthorized parties.

**Fix:** Validate the incoming RMS Bearer token (signature + expiry + issuer claims) against the RMS auth public key before returning the KCB token.
descriptionHtml:
[KNOWN BUG — BUG-OT-002] — CRITICAL SECURITY VULNERABILITY

What this test does:
Sends an intentionally malformed Bearer token ("invalid.token.here") to GET /api/v1/token and verifies the server rejects it with 401.

Expected: HTTP 401 Unauthorized
Actual: HTTP 200 OK — returns a valid KCB API access_token!

Root Cause: The oauth-token service does NOT verify the incoming RMS JWT. It accepts any non-empty string as a Bearer token and returns the KCB API token regardless.

Impact (Critical): The KCB OAuth token is obtainable by any caller who sends any string in the Authorization header. This bypasses the entire authentication layer and exposes a sensitive third-party token to unauthorized parties.

Fix: Validate the incoming RMS Bearer token (signature + expiry + issuer claims) against the RMS auth public key before returning the KCB token.

testStage.description:
**[KNOWN BUG — BUG-OT-002] — CRITICAL SECURITY VULNERABILITY**

**What this test does:**
Sends an intentionally malformed Bearer token ("invalid.token.here") to GET /api/v1/token and verifies the server rejects it with 401.

**Expected:** HTTP 401 Unauthorized
**Actual:** HTTP 200 OK — returns a valid KCB API access_token!

**Root Cause:** The oauth-token service does NOT verify the incoming RMS JWT. It accepts any non-empty string as a Bearer token and returns the KCB API token regardless.

**Impact (Critical):** The KCB OAuth token is obtainable by any caller who sends any string in the Authorization header. This bypasses the entire authentication layer and exposes a sensitive third-party token to unauthorized parties.

**Fix:** Validate the incoming RMS Bearer token (signature + expiry + issuer claims) against the RMS auth public key before returning the KCB token.
